page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m1.large is an x86_64 architecture server powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon CPU running at 2.5 GHz. It features 2 physical cores (2 vCPUs) with 1 thread per core, 33 MB of L3 cache, and 2.0 GB of system memory, yielding 1.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or a GPU, and it operates on a KVM hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate moderate single-core capabilities but limited multi-core and memory scaling. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance (1834.58) and single-threaded gzip compression (27.73 GB/sec) score in the average tier. However, multi-core stress-ng CPU performance (1918.98) and PassMark CPU score (2716.28) fall into the bottom 10% tier. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with large-block read and write speeds registering at 15.64 GB/sec and 13.95 GB/sec respectively, both placing in the bottom 10%.

Due to its dedicated CPU allocation paired with a highly constrained 2.0 GB memory capacity, this instance offers an entry-level hardware profile. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for lightweight, single-threaded tasks that do not require substantial memory or local storage. Appropriate workloads include basic web serving, lightweight background utilities, single-threaded scripting, and simple development or testing environments.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m1.large is an entry-level x86_64 server featuring 2 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs (2 physical cores) operating at 2.5 GHz, paired with 2.0 GB of RAM. Running on a KVM hypervisor, this instance lacks local storage and GPU acceleration, offering a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark data shows average single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, but poor multi-core scaling and constrained memory bandwidth, both scoring in the bottom 10%. With a low 1.0 GB of RAM per core, this server is qualitatively cost-efficient for lightweight, single-threaded workloads. It is best suited for basic web serving, simple scripting, and development environments that do not require high memory capacity or local storage.
